message.content: clear all nextjs turbo and stale pids

input.command: pkill -f "next dev"; pkill -f "next-server"; pkill -f "turbo"; pkill -f "inngest"; pkill -f "ngrok"; sleep 1; ps aux | grep -E "(next|turbo|inngest|ngrok)" | grep -v grep
input.description: Kill Next.js, Turbo, Inngest, ngrok processes

content.text:
Found substantial stale state. Report below — want confirmation before killing MCPs since some may belong to live Claude sessions.

**Orphan MCP servers (18 TTYs, ~60 processes)**
Each old Claude session spawned 3-4 MCP servers (`lightfast core/mcp`, `mcp-server-postgres`, `exa-mcp-server`). Stuck on TTYs: ttys021, 023, 024, 027, 053, 056, 058, 059, 060, 067, 068, 069, 070, 071, 074, 075, 076. Some are from Friday.

**Clean**
- No turbo daemon running
- No inngest CLI running
- No stale `/tmp/*-dev.log` files
- Dev ports (3024/4101/4107/4112) free

**Keep (don't kill)**
- nvim LSP: `tsserver`, `typescript-language-server`, `tailwindcss-language-server`, biome servers
- `agent-browser` (89651)
- Port 5000 is Apple ControlCenter (AirPlay), not dev

Want me to:
1. Kill orphan MCPs from non-current TTYs only? (I'd need to identify the current session's TTY to spare it)
2. Nuke all MCP processes — you can reopen Claude sessions after
3. Leave MCPs, skip this

Also can clear caches if useful: `.next`, `.turbo`, `node_modules/.cache`. Say the word.
